local_names.romanized_local: Shēng měng huó pángxiè tàocān (XL) 2 zhī

food_subgroup: Crustaceans — whole live crab, combo serving

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general crab food concept; no specific entry for a Singaporean live crab combo dish. closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers crabs as a category. No specific code for a combo live crab restaurant dish; closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226955003 is the closest available concept for crab as food; no specific code for a combo restaurant serving.

serving.natural_language_portion: One combo serving consists of 2 whole XL live crabs, typically weighing approximately 800 g total edible and shell weight as served, shared between 2–3 diners.

nutrient_source.title: Crab, cooked, moist heat (Blue crab / mud crab composite) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB No. 15139; supplemented with FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table for crab (cooked)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for a Singaporean live crab combo dish exists in HPB Foodo or USDA. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cooked crab (NDB 15139) and cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA crab (cooked) data. The combo preparation (steamed or stir-fried with light seasoning) is consistent with these reference values. Sodium is elevated slightly to account for soy sauce seasoning typical of this dish style.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; supplemented by University of Sydney GI Database analogous protein-dominant seafood entries
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a Singaporean live crab combo dish. Crab and other cooked crustaceans are high-protein, very low-carbohydrate foods (carbohydrate_g ~0.9 per 100 g). The University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature consistently assign GI values of approximately 5 or below to protein-dominant seafood with negligible carbohydrate content. GI of 5 is used as a conservative analogous estimate consistent with cooked shellfish and crustacean entries. gi_category is low (≤55). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (5 × 7.2) / 100 ≈ 0.4, where carbohydrate per 800 g serving ≈ 7.2 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from soy sauce seasoning
hypertension.guidance: Each 800 g combo serving may deliver approximately 3360 mg sodium when seasoned with soy sauce; individuals with hypertension should request reduced-sauce preparation and limit portion size to one crab.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate content and low glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: With only ~0.9 g carbohydrate per 100 g and a GI of approximately 5, steamed or lightly stir-fried crab has a negligible glycemic impact and is a suitable high-protein, low-GI protein source for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, provided sodium-heavy sauces are minimised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Cholesterol content and sodium from seasoning
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Crab provides beneficial omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids and low saturated fat (~0.4 g/100 g), which are cardioprotective; however, cholesterol (~78 mg/100 g) and high sodium from soy sauce seasoning warrant moderation for individuals with established cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Combo Crab (XL) 2 Pieces (生猛活螃蟹套餐 XL 2只) is a Singaporean Chinese seafood dish featuring two extra-large whole live crabs, typically mud crabs (Scylla serrata) or Sri Lankan crabs, prepared to order at seafood restaurants. The crabs are commonly steamed, stir-fried with ginger and spring onion, or prepared in a light soy-based sauce. The flesh is sweet, tender, and succulent with a firm texture, and the roe (in female crabs) adds a rich, briny flavour. This dish is a premium restaurant offering, priced at SGD 168 per combo at establishments such as The Seafood Market (By House of White Bee Hoon). Nutritionally, crab is an excellent source of high-quality protein (~18 g/100 g), vitamin B12 (~9.8 mcg/100 g), selenium (~36 mcg/100 g), zinc (~3.5 mg/100 g), and phosphorus (~229 mg/100 g), while being low in fat (~1.8 g/100 g) and carbohydrates (~0.9 g/100 g). The glycemic index is very low (estimated ~5), making it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. The primary clinical concern is sodium, which can be elevated significantly by soy sauce and seasoning used in preparation. This dish is not halal-certified in most contexts due to the nature of live crab handling and cross-contamination risks. It is a culturally significant celebratory seafood dish in Singapore, often ordered for family gatherings and special occasions.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a whole crab?
Sodium in crab with soy sauce
Best way to eat crab for weight loss

confidence_reason: No exact entry for a Singaporean live crab combo dish exists in HPB Foodo or any single approved primary source. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cooked crab (NDB 15139) and FAO/INFOODS SEA crab data, which are highly representative of the core ingredient. GI is an analogous estimate from peer-reviewed literature. Confidence is medium rather than high because the exact preparation style (seasoning, sauce quantity) introduces variability not captured by the reference source.
notes: 1. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not consistently reported across the reference sources used (USDA SR Legacy NDB 15139 and FAO/INFOODS SEA); the value is not reliably established for this food in the approved source set. 2. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary reference sources used. 3. Trans fat (trans_fat_g) is 0.0; confirmed zero for cooked crab per USDA SR Legacy — naturally zero as crab does not contain industrially produced trans fats. 4. Sugar (sugar_g) is 0.0; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for cooked crab — naturally zero as crab flesh contains no free sugars. 5. Dietary fibre (dietary_fibre_g) is 0.0; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy — naturally zero as crab is an animal-derived food with no plant fibre. 6. Sodium value of 420 mg/100 g is an adapted estimate accounting for light soy sauce seasoning typical of this dish; plain cooked crab per USDA is approximately 293 mg/100 g. 7. GI of 5 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific dish exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 8. This dish is not halal-certified at the listed restaurant. 9. The combo serving size of 800 g is an estimate for two XL whole crabs as served; actual edible yield is approximately 30–40% of whole weight.
